metadata.name: ario_ant_escrow
metadata.description: AR.IO Trustless ANT Escrow Program - Multi-protocol (Arweave RSA-PSS-4096 / Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1) on-chain custody for Metaplex Core ANT NFTs

docs: AR.IO Trustless ANT Escrow Program
Holds Metaplex Core ANT NFTs in a per-mint PDA and releases them to a
claimant designated by an Arweave RSA-PSS-4096 or Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1
signature verified fully on-chain. No off-chain authority, no oracle.
See `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md` for the full technical design and
`docs/ANT_ESCROW_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` for the phase-by-phase roadmap.

instructions.name: cancel_deposit
docs: Return the escrowed ANT to the depositor and close the escrow PDA.
Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow account being closed. `has_one = depositor` rejects calls
from anyone other than the original depositor; `close = depositor`
returns rent and zeroes the data on success.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset returning to the depositor.
`has_one`-style validation: `escrow.ant_mint` must equal this
account's key. Defends against wallet UIs that mis-route accounts.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor — sole authority for cancel. Must sign so the
system program will credit them with the closed-account rent.

instructions.name: claim_ant_arweave
docs: Release the ANT to `claimant` after on-chain RSA-PSS-4096
verification. Anyone can submit; only the named claimant receives
the asset. See `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md` § Cryptographic
verification → Arweave RSA-PSS for the algorithm.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA. `has_one = depositor` ties the close-rent recipient to
the original depositor account passed below; `close = depositor`
returns rent on successful claim. Cached `bump` is re-derived via
the seeds constraint for defense-in-depth.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset being released. Must match
`escrow.ant_mint` so a misrouted account can't redirect the
transfer.

accounts.name: claimant
docs: Recipient of the ANT — its pubkey is bound into the canonical
message that the Arweave key signed. Front-runners can resubmit
the tx but cannot redirect the asset.
no on-chain ownership check needed (anyone can be a claimant).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor — receives rent on escrow close. Pinned via
`has_one = depositor` on the escrow above.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Tx fee payer. Doesn't have to be the claimant — anyone can submit
a valid claim signature on the recipient's behalf.

instructions.name: claim_ant_ethereum
docs: Release the ANT to `claimant` after on-chain ECDSA secp256k1 +
EIP-191 verification. Same shape as `claim_ant_arweave`, only the
signature format and verifier differ.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA. Same constraints as `ClaimAntArweave`.

instructions.name: deposit_ant
docs: Lock an ANT into escrow targeted at an Arweave or Ethereum identity.
`recipient_protocol`: `0` = Arweave RSA-PSS-4096, `1` = Ethereum ECDSA.
`recipient_pubkey`: 512-byte RSA modulus (Arweave) or 20-byte address
(Ethereum); any other (protocol, length) pair is rejected.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: New escrow PDA, one per ANT mint. `init` enforces "no double
deposit" — a second `deposit_ant` for the same mint fails because
the PDA already exists.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset (ANT) being escrowed. Constrained to the
mpl-core program so callers can't pass a system account or a
different program's NFT. Inner-data discriminator is checked in
the handler via `read_mpl_core_owner`.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Caller — must be the current ANT owner. Pays rent + tx fee + signs
the mpl-core TransferV1 CPI.

accounts.name: mpl_core_program
docs: Metaplex Core program. Pinned to MPL_CORE_PROGRAM_ID so the CPI
can't be redirected.

instructions.name: update_recipient
docs: Re-target the escrow at a different recipient identity. Rotates the
nonce so any in-flight claim sigs bound to the prior recipient are
invalidated. Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow being re-targeted. `has_one = depositor` enforces the
authority check and the `seeds`/`bump = escrow.bump` constraint
re-derives the PDA against the cached bump (cheaper than
`find_program_address`).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor — sole authority for `update_recipient`.

types.name: EscrowAnt
docs: Per-escrow custody record. One PDA per escrowed ANT, derived from
`[ESCROW_ANT_SEED, ant_mint]`. Layout matches `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md`
§ Account model byte-for-byte (653 bytes payload + 8-byte Anchor
discriminator = 661 bytes on-chain).
The `_reserved` tail and the explicit `version` byte are forward-compat
hooks so additive field changes can ship without a `realloc` migration.

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version. v1 = `ESCROW_VERSION_V1`.

fields.name: bump
docs: Cached PDA bump. Faster than `find_program_address` on signer-seed
reconstruction during transfer CPIs.

fields.name: depositor
docs: Wallet that called `deposit_ant`. Sole authority for
`cancel_deposit` and `update_recipient`; receives rent on close.

fields.name: ant_mint
docs: Metaplex Core asset (ANT) mint pubkey held in custody.
Denormalized from PDA seeds for defense-in-depth account validation.

fields.name: recipient_protocol
docs: `0` = Arweave RSA-PSS-4096, `1` = Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey_len
docs: Active length (in bytes) of the recipient pubkey blob below.
Constrained at write time to {512, 20} per protocol.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey
docs: Recipient identity bytes, zero-padded to `RECIPIENT_PUBKEY_MAX_LEN`.
- Arweave: 512-byte big-endian RSA modulus (the JWK `n` field).
- Ethereum: 20-byte Keccak address followed by 492 zero bytes.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Anti-replay nonce. Initial value:
`sha256(deposit_slot_le_bytes || ant_mint || depositor)`
Rotated by `update_recipient` to invalidate any in-flight claim sigs.

fields.name: deposit_slot
docs: Slot at deposit. Informational only — used as a salt for nonce
derivation but not consulted on claim. Useful for indexers and
support tooling.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Reserved bytes. Hold zero until repurposed in a future schema bump.
